Shot: Definition: How often player looks to finish possessions with a shot (usage tendency).
Touch: Definition: How often offense flows through player (touches/initiations/handoffs/resets/play creations).
Shot Close: Definition: Frequency of shots from ~3–10 feet (push shots, short hooks, short fades).
Shot Under: Definition: Frequency of point-blank/rim finishes (layups, dunks, drop-offs).
Shot Mid: Definition: Overall willingness to take mid-range jumpers (any context).
Off-Screen Mid: Definition: Willingness to shoot mid-range immediately after coming off a screen.
Shot 3: Definition: Overall willingness to take 3PT jumpers.
Spot-Up 3: Definition: Catch-and-shoot 3PT willingness when feet are set.
Off-Screen 3: Definition: Willingness to shoot immediately after coming off a screen into a 3.
Contested Mid: Definition: Willingness to take mid-range jumpers with a defender close/contesting.
Contested 3: Definition: Willingness to take contested 3PT jumpers.
Step-Back Mid: Definition: Use of step-back move into a mid-range jumper.
Step-Back 3: Definition: Use of step-back move into a 3PT jumper.
Spin Jumper: Definition: Use of spin move into a jumper (usually mid-range).
Transition Pull-Up 3: Definition: Pull-up 3PT taken in transition (early offense).
Dribble Pull-Up Mid: Definition: Pull-up mid-range jumper off dribble in halfcourt.
Dribble Pull-Up 3: Definition: Pull-up 3PT off dribble in halfcourt.
Drive: Definition: Overall willingness to attack off the dribble to the rim (downhill).
Spot-Up Drive: Definition: Willingness to attack a closeout off a catch (one or two dribbles).
Off-Screen Drive: Definition: Willingness to curl/attack after receiving ball off an off-ball screen.
Use Glass: Definition: Preference to finish layups off the backboard when applicable.
Step Through: Definition: Use of step-through footwork to finish around defenders. Mostly bigs and crafty finishers.
Spin Layup: Definition: Use of spin layup gather/finish.
Eurostep: Definition: Use of eurostep to avoid contact at rim.
Hop Step: Definition: gathered-move used to create space, avoid defenders, or reset your footing for a shot.
Floater: Definition: most effective finishing tools, especially for smaller guards or forwards who need to score over rim protectors
Standing Dunk: Definition: ultimate power move for interior players. It allows a player to explode directly from a stationary position under the rim into a dunk, bypassing the need for a "run-up" or momentum.
Driving Dunk: Definition: premier scoring tool for slashers. It represents a player's ability to explode toward the rim from a run and finish over defenders.
Flashy Dunk: Definition: ultimate tool for style and momentum.
Alley-Oop: Definition: How often a player attempts to throw lob passes to a teammate near the rim
Putback: Definition: How often a player attempts to immediately score after an offensive rebound
Crash: Definition: willingness to acccept collsion bumps while attackining the basket. collision intent
Drive Right: Definition: drive bias to the right.
Drive Step Back: Definition: a drible step back move to create separation for a shot or drive to basket
Drive Behind Back: Definition: use of behind the back dribble while driving to change direction/protect ball
Drive Hesitation: Definition: use of dribble hesi to pause or freeze defender before attack to basket
Drive In & Out: Definition: using hesi/in and out dribble moves to freeze a defender before attacking the basket
No Drive Dribble Move: Definition: avoid using drible moves when attacking the basket
Attack Strong Drive: Definition: how hard a player commits to finishing through contact once the drive starts to the basket
Dish: Definition: pass out of a drive or passes to open men
Flashy Pass: Definition: Frequency of using risky/high style passes
Alley-Oop Pass: Definition: Frequency of attempting lob passes to cutters/rollers
Roll vs Pop: Definition: What a screener does after a set screen
ISO vs Elite: Definition: the ultimate measure of a player's "fearlessness" and offensive confidence. It dictates how often an AI-controlled player will initiate a one-on-one isolation play when they are being guarded by a Lockdown Defender (DPOY candidates or players with high-tier defensive badges).
ISO vs Good: Definition: controls how often an AI-controlled player will initiate a one-on-one "Isolation" play when they are being guarded by a defender with a solid/high defensive rating.
ISO vs Average: Definition: dictates how often an AI-controlled player will break off a set play to go one-on-one against a defender with a standard/median defensive rating.
ISO vs Poor: Definition: specific player tendency that dictates how often an AI-controlled player will choose to "Isolate" (go one-on-one) specifically when they are being guarded by a defender with a Poor defensive rating.
Play Discipline: Definition: It determines how strictly a player/AI follows a called play before "breaking" it to improvise (taking a shot or driving).
Post Up: Definition: the state of backing down a defender with your back to the basket.
Post Back Down: Definition: fundamental action of using your player's body to push a defender toward the hoop while your back is to the basket
Post Aggressive Back Down: Definition: power move where your player uses their full body weight and strength to shove a defender deeper into the paint.
Post Face Up: Definition: transition where your player stops backing down the defender (butt-to-basket) and turns around to look them in the eye (chest-to-basket).
Post Spin: Definition: Quick, circular move used to whip around a defender while you are backing them down. It is one of the most effective ways for agile bigs and strong guards to get a clear path to the rim.
Post Drive: Definition: move used to explode out of a post-up and head directly toward the basket for a layup or dunk
Post Drop Step: Definition: Post Drop Step is a powerful interior move used to "seal" your defender and spin toward the basket for a high-percentage layup or dunk.
Shoot From Post: Definition: shooting from the post is a deep system that combines your Mid-Range attribute (for fades/hops) and your Close Shot attribute (for hooks/layups).
Post Hook Left: Definition: Interior scoring move where your player uses their left hand to "hook" the ball over the defender while backing them down.
Post Hook Right: Definition: Interior scoring move where your player uses their right hand to "hook" the ball over the defender while backing them down.
Post Fade Left: Definition: scoring move where your player, while backing down a defender, spins over their left shoulder to create separation for a jump shot.
Post Fade Right: Definition: scoring move where your player backs down a defender and then turns over their right shoulder to fade away for a jumper.
Post Shimmy: Definition: deceptive scoring move where your player performs a shoulder fake or "wiggle" before transitioning into a shot (either a hook or a fadeaway).
Post Hop Shot: Definition: scoring move used while backing down a defender in the post. It involves your player "hopping" away from the basket or to the side to create space before launching a jumper.
Post Step Back: Definition: how frequently a player, while backed down in the post, will suddenly spring backward away from the hoop to create space for a jumper.
Post Up & Under: Definition: specifically controls how often a player uses a fake from a post position to bait the defender into jumping or leaning, followed by a step-around or "under" move for a layup or hook shot.
Take Charge: Definition: Willingness to attempt drawing charges. Gameplay: too high can create unrealistic charge hunting.
Foul: Definition: General foul tendency (frequency of committing fouls). Lower is safer. Use with Hard Foul to control aggression without whistle spam.
Hard Foul: Definition: Likelihood of hard fouls (strong contact / wrap-ups). Keep low for realism; mostly for enforcers/bigs.
Pass Interception: Definition: Aggressiveness jumping lanes / intercepting passes (off-ball). Too high can create teleport steals if gameplay sliders also high.
On-Ball Steal: Definition: Aggressiveness reaching for steals on-ball. Balance with foul rates; high reach increases fouls/whiffs.
Block: Definition: Aggressiveness attempting blocks at the rim. Too high can create jumpy help and leave glass exposed.
Contest Shot: Definition: Aggressiveness contesting shots (closeouts, hands up, challenges).  Pairs with gameplay Help Defense + Contest sliders; avoid stacking extremes.
